t_ch: 你们不是在追「冷面小青龙」吗？
t_en: Aren't you fans of Cold Dragon Young?

t_ch: 对，我们刚才是在聊这个。
t_en: Yeah, we were just talking about him.

t_ch: 怎么，你也是自由搏击圈的？那你应该也对小青龙有所了解吧？
t_en: So? Are you also into mixed martial arts? Then you must know about Cold Dragon Young, right?

t_ch: 我听到你们说话了。
t_en: I heard you talking.

t_ch: 呃…所以呢？
t_en: Uh... So?

t_ch: 哦，我明白了——你也是混自由搏击圈的，是吧？那你应该也听过那位小青龙的名号吧？
t_en: Oh, I see. You're also involved in mixed martial arts, yeah? So, you've probably heard of Cold Dragon Young, right?

t_ch: 有人说那位选手只是昙花一现，但我俩都不这么觉得。迟早有一天，「冷面小青龙」会卷土重来，靠着硬实力席卷磐岩镇的搏击圈……
t_en: Some say that fighter was a one-hit wonder, but we don't think so. Someday, Cold Dragon Young will come roaring back and take Boulder Town's mixed martial arts circle by storm...

t_ch: …咳，不好意思。一聊到这个，我的话匣子就被打开了。你刚才是想给我们看什么东西，对吧？
t_en: ...Ahem. Sorry. Once I start talking shop, I just can't get my chatterbox to stop. What did you want me to look at for you?

t_ch: （给两人看丹恒的照片。）
t_en: (Show them a picture of Dan Heng.)

t_ch: 这…兄弟你看，这是——
t_en: This is... bro, check it out. This is —

t_ch: 嗯…不会错的，绝对是「冷面小青龙」本人的肖像。而且图像相当清晰，看来是出自专业人士之手……
t_en: Uh-huh... No doubt about it. This is definitely a portrait of Cold Dragon Young. The image is pretty clear, too. It looks like it's done by a professional...

t_ch: 这…这照片肯定值个好价，以后还有升值空间！你是从哪儿搞到的？
t_en: This... This picture has got to be worth quite a bit, and the price will only go up later! Where did you get your hands on this?

t_ch: 我自有渠道。
t_en: I've got my connections.

t_ch: 不方便透露。
t_en: That's for me to know.

t_ch: 呃…理解，有这么好的路子，你不想轻易松口也是自然。
t_en: Hmm... I understand. With connections that can get you something this good, it's only natural you'd want to keep it to yourself.

t_ch: 怎么说，兄弟？依我看，咱们不如赶紧出钱拿下——免得等到以后价格飞涨，我们只能躲在角落里暗自后悔……
t_en: What do you say, bro? The way I see it, we should put the money down now. If we wait to buy it after the price has gone up, we'll only have ourselves to blame...

t_ch: 嗯…有道理。这位{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}，你愿意把这张照片卖给我们吗？
t_en: Hmm... That makes sense. {F#Sister}{M#Brother}, are you willing to sell this picture to us?

t_ch: 送给你们吧。
t_en: I'll give it to you.

t_ch: …送、送给我们？
t_en: ...G—Give it to us?

t_ch: 这样真的好吗？这磐岩镇上居然还有这种大善人，实在有点让人难以置信啊……
t_en: Is that really OK? Who knew that Boulder Town still has such saintly souls?

t_ch: 拿着吧，我能复制出无数份。
t_en: Take it. I can make tons of copies.

t_ch: 收下吧，这里没有阴谋。
t_en: It's yours. No tricks.

t_ch: …那，那我们就收下了。
t_en: ...Well. I guess we'll take it.

t_ch: 多谢了，慷慨大方的{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}！等小青龙的周边涨价了，我们肯定会念得你的好的。
t_en: Thank you, my generous {F#sister}{M#brother}. When the price of Cold Dragon Young's merchandise goes up, we will be sure to remember your kindness.

t_ch: <unbreak>3000</unbreak>信用点。
t_en: <unbreak>3000</unbreak> credits.

t_ch: 唔，<unbreak>3000</unbreak>…倒也说不上多贵。
t_en: Oh, <unbreak>3000</unbreak>... That's not too expensive.

t_ch: 你怎么看，兄弟？
t_en: What do you say, bro?

t_ch: 嗯…咱们一人垫一半吧。这种机会可不是天天都能碰上的。
t_en: Hmmm... We'll each kick in half. This kind of opportunity doesn't come around every day.

t_ch: 那我们就收下了，朋友——这是你的钱，拿好。
t_en: We'll take it, friend. Here's your money.

t_ch: 你开的价还挺公道的…等小青龙的周边涨价了，我们会念得你的好的，{F#姐妹}{M#兄弟}。
t_en: You gave us a fair deal... When the price of Cold Dragon Young's merchandise goes up, we'll remember your kindness, {F#sister}{M#brother}.

t_ch: 瞧一瞧，看一看！
t_en: Come and feast your eyes on this!

t_ch: 磐岩镇名吃，炸岩居蟹！
t_en: Boulder Town's famous dish, Deep-fried Rock Crab!

t_ch: 还有大胃王挑战赛——
t_en: We also have the Competitive Eater Championship!

t_ch: ——赢家免单！没错，免单！
t_en: Winners eat free! That's right. I said, "Free!"

t_ch: 瞧一瞧，看一看啊！
t_en: Come and feast your eyes!

t_ch: 唷，客人，欢迎光临！想来点什么？
t_en: Well, hello! Welcome! What would you like to order?

t_ch: 咱这儿有杂菜汤，炸岩居蟹，盐烧洞螈，宝石蜥肉串……
t_en: We have Wild Herb Soup, Deep-fried Rock Crab, Salt Grilled Olm, Crystal Lizard Satay...

t_ch: 给我讲讲这个「大胃王挑战赛」。
t_en: Tell me about this Competitive Eater Championship.

t_ch: …喔，原来如此，你是为这个来的啊！
t_en: ...Oh. I see. That's what you're here for!

t_ch: 告诉你吧，规则很简单：看见这几盘炸好的岩居蟹了吗？等我开始掐表，您就使劲把蟹肉往自个儿嘴里塞——只要能在<unbreak>20</unbreak>秒内吃下5盘，本摊位全场对你免单！
t_en: Let me lay it out for you. The rules are simple. See these plates of already fried Rock Crabs? As soon as I start the clock, you need to do everything you can to stuff all that crab meat into your mouth. If you get five plates knocked out in twenty seconds, then everything in this stall is free for you to enjoy!

t_ch: 怎么样，打算参加吗？
t_en: What do you think? Want to give it a go?

t_ch: 我准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 好嘞——瞧你这状态，自信满满！记得备瓶水，可别被咱家的大螃蟹噎着了！
t_en: That's what I'm talking about! Just looking at you, I can tell you've got confidence to spare! Remember to get a bottle of water. You don't want to choke on a big hunk of crab!

t_ch: 您准备好了就到摊车前面来吧，挑战随时开始！
t_en: Come to the front of the stall when you are ready. The challenge will start whenever you want!

t_ch: 我得再到别处热热身…
t_en: I need to step away for a moment to warm up...

t_ch: 没问题！只要我这小摊还没打烊，您随时可以回来挑战。
t_en: No problem! You can come back and start the challenge anytime, so long as my stall is open.

t_ch: 回头见，伙计！
t_en: See ya later, pal!

t_ch: 镇民们，乡亲们！瞧一瞧，看一看！又一位勇士要来挑战磐岩镇大胃王的头衔了！
t_en: Come one, and come all! Feast your eyes on this! We have another brave soul who has come to take on the challenge for the title of Boulder Town's Competitive Eater Champion.

t_ch: 这家伙能行吗？表情倒挺自信的。
t_en: Do they have what it takes? Their expression at least looks confident.

t_ch: 开玩笑吧？你看{F#她}{M#他}瘦得跟杆子一样……
t_en: You must be joking? Look, {F#she}{M#he} is as skinny as a bean pole...

t_ch: …勇敢的挑战者，告诉大家，你叫什么名字？
t_en: ...Courageous challenger, tell us, what is your name?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}。
t_en: {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}——记住这个名字，短短几十秒过后，{F#她}{M#他}可能就会成为万人景仰的磐岩镇大胃王！
t_en: {NICKNAME} — remember this name. In just a moment, {F#she}{M#he} could very well be Boulder Town's Competitive Eater Champion worthy of our admiration!

t_ch: （思考片刻）…叫我「叮蟹」就好。
t_en: (Thinks for a moment) ...Call me "the Stinging Crab."

t_ch: 哇哦，听听！这世上还有更巧的事吗？这位挑战者居然都把「蟹」字写进名字里了！
t_en: Wow. Did you hear that? Has there ever been a greater coincidence? The challenger made the word "crab" part of their name!

t_ch: 「叮蟹」——记住这个名字，短短几十秒过后，{F#她}{M#他}可能就会成为万人景仰的磐岩镇大胃王！
t_en: "The Stinging Crab" — remember this name. In just a moment, {F#she}{M#he} very well could be Boulder Town's Competitive Eater Champion worthy of our admiration!

t_ch: 准备好了吗？我会先倒数三个数，然后开始计时——记住，得在<unbreak>20</unbreak>秒内吃下5盘炸岩居蟹才算挑战成功！
t_en: Are you ready? I will count down from three, then start the clock. Don't forget. The challenge is only considered complete when you eat five plates of Deep-fried Rock Crabs in twenty seconds!

t_ch: 三…二…一……
t_en: Three... Two... One...

t_ch: …开始！
吃第一口。
t_en: Take the first bite.

t_ch: 鲜香、浓郁的味道在你口中晕开。你完全未曾想过，自己居然能在下层区品尝到如此的美味。
t_en: You are blown away by the fresh, fragrant, and rich flavor that hits your mouth. You've never imagined that you could enjoy such a delectable flavor in the Underworld.

t_ch: 吃第二口。
t_en: Take the second bite.

t_ch: 第二口蟹肉…你的嘴巴已经被填满了。有些油腻，但还算得上美味。你确信自己能完成这次挑战。
t_en: The second bite of crab meat fills up all of your mouth. It's a little greasy, but you would still consider it tasty. You are certain that you can see this challenge through to the end.

t_ch: 吃第三口。
t_en: Take the third bite.

t_ch: 第三口…该死的添加剂，你感到口渴和恶心。此外，你还在心中暗骂——凭什么设置里没有加快文字展示速度的选项？
t_en: The third bite... These stupid additives. You feel thirsty and nauseous. You also complain internally about having no option in the settings to speed up the text display.

t_ch: 吃…第四…口…
t_en: Take... the fourth... bite...

t_ch: 你还有心情读这些文字？
t_en: You can still stomach reading this text???

t_ch: 好吧，偷偷告诉你，岩居蟹不属于十足目，它是一种——根据宇宙普遍的物种分类法——难以被归类的近似节肢生物。管它呢？反正是螃蟹就会吐泡泡，就像你现在也临近呕吐的边缘……
t_en: Alright. I'll tell you a little something. The Rock Crab isn't a decapod. It's actually a type of creature that — according to the common species classification system — is difficult to classify, but it's something like an arthropod. Who cares? It's a crab that spits bubbles, just like how you are on the verge of spitting up everything you've just eaten...

t_ch: 最…后…一…口…
t_en: The... final... bite...

t_ch: 你的脑海一片空白；你的胃是被几兆亿吨蟹膏填满的大海。
t_en: Your mind is completely blank. Your stomach is a vast sea filled with trillions of tons of crab paste.

t_ch: 不可思议…太精彩了！人不可貌相——别看{F#姐们儿}{M#哥们儿}的体型这么纤弱，我猜他肚子里有九成的空间都是给胃腾出来的！
t_en: Mindblowing... That was incredible! You really can't judge a book by its cover. We shouldn't be looking at how frail our {F#gal's}{M#dude's} body is. I reckon <unbreak>90%</unbreak> of {F#her}{M#his} body is just the stomach!

t_ch: 摊主的赞扬传进你的耳朵…但你此刻无暇接受任何人的吹捧。你只想做一件事……
t_en: The stall owner's praise reaches your ears, but you don't have the mental space to take it in. All you can think about is one thing...

t_ch: 喂，你瞧呐！那家伙八成是要吐了。
t_en: Hey, look! {F#She}{M#He} is gonna throw up.

t_ch: 哈哈，这下有好戏看了！
t_en: Haha. We've got a good show coming up!
*barfs*...

t_ch: …你没事吧，{F#姐们儿}{M#哥们儿}？
t_en: ...Going to be all right, {F#sister}{M#brother}?

t_ch: 糟透了…
t_en: I feel awful...

t_ch: 哈，谁都能看出来！但是没关系——因为你赢了！
t_en: Ha! Anyone with eyes can see that! You'll be fine. You're a winner!

t_ch: 我赢了…
t_en: I won...

t_ch: 没错，伙计！你赢了，赢得光明正大！
t_en: That's right, friend! Your victory was glorious!

t_ch: 从现在起，本摊位的所有小吃对您免费——您的名字还将被记上「大胃王」名人堂…这可是莫大的荣誉！
t_en: From this moment forward, all of this stall's snacks are free for you. Your name will be added to the Competitive Eater Championship's Hall of Fame... This is a huge honor!

t_ch: 哦，对了…我这儿还准备了一些特别的小礼品，请您一并收下！
t_en: Oh, right... I prepared a few special gifts for you. Here you go!

t_ch: 很遗憾…您虽然成功吃下了5盘蟹肉，但是<unbreak>20</unbreak>秒的挑战时间已经超时——
t_en: You did manage to eat all five plates of crab meat, but unfortunately... you went over twenty seconds.

t_ch: 摊主的话语传进你的耳朵…但你此刻已经意识模糊，根本无暇考虑输赢。你只想做一件事……
t_en: The stall owner's words reach your ears, but... you are already slipping into unconsciousness. You couldn't care about whether you won or lost. There's only one thing on your mind...

t_ch: 喂，你瞧呐！那家伙八成是要吐了。
t_en: Hey, look! {F#She}{M#He} is gonna throw up.

t_ch: 哈哈，这下有好戏看了！
t_en: Haha. We've got a good show coming up!
*barfs*...

t_ch: …你没事吧，{F#姐们儿}{M#哥们儿}？
t_en: ...Going to be all right, {F#sister}{M#brother}?

t_ch: 糟透了…
t_en: I feel awful...

t_ch: 理解，我理解…太不幸了！你离胜利就差那么一点点……
t_en: I get it. I really do... What a shame! You were right on the cusp of victory...

t_ch: 我输了…
t_en: I lost...

t_ch: …对，伙计，太倒霉了！你离胜利就差那么几秒钟……
t_en: ...Yes, my friend. Quite unfortunate! You were just a few seconds off from victory.

t_ch: 那个…{F#姐们儿}{M#哥们儿}，我知道你现在感觉很糟……
t_en: So... {F#sister}{M#brother}, I know that you feel terrible right now...

t_ch: 不过根据规则，挑战没成功就不能免单。所以，五盘炸岩居蟹的价格一共是——<unbreak>1000</unbreak>信用点。您是想现在支付，还是……
t_en: However, according to the rules, contestants who don't complete the challenge must pay for their meals. With that in mind, five plates of Deep-Fried Rock Crab cost <unbreak>1000</unbreak> credits. Would you like to pay now, or...

t_ch: …认赌服输。我这就交钱。
t_en: ...I admit defeat. I'll pay now.

t_ch: 爽气！您这么守规矩的客人，这年头真是越来越少了…我记住您了，下次光临本摊绝对给您一个超低折扣！
t_en: Nice! You are a customer who sticks to the rules. Quite a rarity these days... I'll remember you. Next time you stop by, I absolutely will give you an incredible discount!

t_ch: 多谢惠顾，伙计！
t_en: Thanks for your business, pal!

t_ch: 哦，对了——你如果还想参加磐岩镇大胃王挑战的话，不妨明天再来咱的摊位看看。我保证，以你这优秀的底子，肯定用不了几次就能挑战成功！
t_en: Oh, right. If you want to participate in the Boulder Town's Competitive Eater Championship again, feel free to come by tomorrow. With your excellent ability, I guarantee that you won't need more than a few tries to beat the challenge!

t_ch: 奸商…
t_en: What a sly merchant...

t_ch: …哎呀，怎么能这么说呢？规则我也跟你说过了，咱也是要做生意的嘛……
t_en: ...Hey, now. What's all that about? I told you the rules upfront. I need to earn a living too...

t_ch: 再说了，看{F#姐们儿}{M#哥们儿}这一身光鲜亮丽的打扮…<unbreak>1000</unbreak>信用点肯定算不得什么高消费吧？
t_en: Besides, look at you, {F#sister}{M#brother}. You're wearing such glamorous clothes... <unbreak>1000</unbreak> credits is probably nothing to you, right?

t_ch: 多谢惠顾，伙计！
t_en: Thanks for your business, pal!

t_ch: 哦，对了——你如果还想参加磐岩镇大胃王挑战的话，不妨明天再来咱的摊位看看。我保证，以你这优秀的底子，肯定用不了几次就能闯关成功！
t_en: Oh, right. If you want to participate in the Boulder Town's Competitive Eater Championship again, feel free to come by tomorrow. With your excellent ability, you won't need more than a few tries to beat the challenge!

t_ch: 之前说好的免费小食…
t_en: Those free snacks that you've mentioned before...

t_ch: 当然，当然！咱做生意讲的就是个诚信。您瞧瞧，这份才刚出锅——您拿好嘞，慢慢享用！
t_en: Of course, oh yes! We do business here with integrity. Look here, this one's fresh out of the pan — have a bite, and enjoy!

t_ch: 呃，您瞧，我这菜单上所有小食都给您免费尝过一遍了……
t_en: Uh, look — I've given you a free sample of one of everything on the menu here...

t_ch: 咱这是做生意，又不是搞慈善，是吧？还望您见谅……
t_en: We're a business, not a charity, right? Hope you can forgive us...

t_ch: 你好。
t_en: Hello.

t_ch: 哦…你也是来试运气的吗？
t_en: Ah... Are you here to try your luck, too?

t_ch: 运气？
t_en: Luck?

t_ch: 对。运气。
t_en: That's right. Luck.

t_ch: 强者才不需要运气。
t_en: The strong have no need for luck.

t_ch: 吁…走着瞧。
t_en: Hmm... We'll see about that.

t_ch: 你在说什么？
t_en: What are you talking about?

t_ch: 要我重复一遍？
t_en: Do I need to repeat myself?

t_ch: 「你也是来试运气的吗」？
t_en: "Are you here to try your luck, too?"

t_ch: 旁边两个箱子，看见没有？
t_en: Do you see the two chests over there?

t_ch: 一个装着「幸运」，一个装着「不幸」。
t_en: One contains good luck, while the other contains bad luck.

t_ch: 你去打开其中一个。看看今天走不走运。
t_en: Open one of these chests and see if you are lucky today.

t_ch: 「幸运」的箱子里有什么？
t_en: What's inside the chest with good luck?

t_ch: 不…你没理解。
t_en: No... You don't understand.

t_ch: 「不幸」的箱子里有什么？
t_en: What's inside the chest with bad luck?

t_ch: 不…你没理解。
t_en: No... You don't understand.

t_ch: 这是什么新型骗术吗…
t_en: Is this some kind of new con...?

t_ch: …骗术？
t_en: ...a con?

t_ch: 可笑…不，你没理解。
t_en: You're funny... No, you don't understand.

t_ch: 赌局的魅力就是不可知。一切权重都被押在选择的过程上。
t_en: The unknown is what makes gambles so alluring. All of the weight lies on the decision-making process.

t_ch: 「幸」或「不幸」…都是绝对的私人体验。他者无权干涉。
t_en: Good or bad luck... is all a matter of personal experience. No one has the right to interfere.

t_ch: 选吧。
t_en: Make your choice.

t_ch: 打开小型宝箱。
t_en: Open the small chest.

t_ch: 你打开了小宝箱，木板腐朽的气味扑鼻而来。
t_en: You open the small chest. The smell of rotten wood pervades your nostrils.

t_ch: 宝箱内是一方黑暗，衬得角落里闪现的一簇光芒格外惹眼。
t_en: The inside of the chest is completely black, but in the corner of the chest's lining, you see a flash of light that particularly catches your eye.

t_ch: 拿起那个发光的东西。
t_en: Pick up the glowing object.

t_ch: 这是「幸运」还是「不幸」？
t_en: Is it good luck or bad luck?

t_ch: 你把目光投向旁边的女人。但她面无表情，你从那张脸上读不出任何提示。
t_en: You look toward the lady off to the side. Her face is expressionless. You can't get a single hint from her.

t_ch: 管它的…先拿走再说。
t_en: Whatever... Grab it and see what happens.

t_ch: 你将颤抖的手伸入一片漆黑中，抓住了那个——不对，是那一堆——坚硬的、发光的东西。
t_en: Your hands tremble as you reach into the pitch-black darkness. You take hold of a single — no, a pile of rigid, shining objects.

t_ch: 你将手举到眼前，仔细端详掌心里那一撮五颜六色的晶石。
t_en: You bring your hand up to your eyes and closely examine the multicolored crystals in your palm.

t_ch: ——如果这些漂亮的小石头代表的不是「幸运」，那你的价值观真的会被彻底颠覆。
t_en: If these beautiful little stones don't represent good luck, then your values would be completely overturned.

t_ch: 这算「幸运」吗？
t_en: This is good luck, right?

t_ch: 很好。选得很好。
t_en: Good. You chose well.

t_ch: 今天的你得到了「幸运」的青睐…这是莫大的馈赠。
t_en: Today, you have received fortune's favor... This is a great gift.

t_ch: 记住…不要轻视自己的选择。任何选择。
t_en: Remember... never forget the importance of your decisions. Any decision.

t_ch: （不再发问。）
t_en: (Don't ask any more questions.)

t_ch: 女人的眼神中透露出欣慰…但她的眼角很快又恢复了平整。
t_en: The lady's eyes express relief and satisfaction... but her expression quickly returns to normal.

t_ch: 我从你的沉默里察觉到了明智。
t_en: From your silence, I detect wisdom.

t_ch: …很好。
t_en: ...That's good.

t_ch: 另外一个箱子里有什么？
t_en: What's inside the other chest?

t_ch: 女人给了你一个冷酷的眼神…但她的眼角很快又恢复了平整。
t_en: The lady pierces you with an icy glare... but her expression quickly returns to normal.

t_ch: 你今天已经走过一次运了。
t_en: You have been lucky once today already.

t_ch: …不要随便摆弄自己的命运。
t_en: ...Do not trifle with your own fate.

t_ch: 也包括现在这个选择吗？
t_en: Does that also include this choice right now?

t_ch: 女人露出了一个狡黠的眼神…但她的眼角很快又恢复了平整。
t_en: The lady gives you a sly look... but her expression quickly returns to normal.

t_ch: 学得很快……
t_en: You learn fast...

t_ch: 但愿你能记住今天学到的东西。
t_en: May you always remember what you have learned today.

t_ch: 看你做选择相当有趣…过程值得我细品一番。
t_en: Watching you make decisions is fascinating... The process is worth savoring.

t_ch: …有缘再见。
t_en: ...Until we meet again.

t_ch: 你只是一眨眼…那个神秘的女人就从眼前消失了。连一根发丝都没落下。
t_en: You blink and she's... gone. The mysterious woman has disappeared before your very eyes, vanishing without a trace.

t_ch: 真是一次…神奇的相遇。
t_en: This really was... an unusual encounter.

t_ch: 但那个宝箱还在旁边，哪儿都没去。那个可能藏匿着「不幸」的容器……
t_en: But the chest is still there. It hasn't gone anywhere. There it is, a container that conceals bad luck...

t_ch: 该怎么做？选择在你手上，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: What should you do? The choice is yours, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 调查大型宝箱
t_en: Investigate the large chest.

t_ch: 这就是女人口中装着「不幸」的宝箱……
t_en: This is the chest that as the lady said contains bad luck...

t_ch: 看上去和你在探险途中遇到的宝箱区别不大。也许那个女人只是在故弄玄虚，也许……
t_en: It looks pretty much like the chests you've come across while exploring. Maybe that woman was trying to trick you...

t_ch: …可万一她说的是真的呢？
t_en: ...But what if she was telling the truth?

t_ch: 选择在你手上。
t_en: The choice is yours.

t_ch: 打开大宝箱。
t_en: Open the large chest.

t_ch: 静静走开。
t_en: Walk away.

t_ch: 你决定离开。你诚心地希望这是你今天做出的最明智的决定。
t_en: You decide to leave. You earnestly hope that is the wisest decision you've made today.

t_ch: 诱惑是魔鬼——今天你战胜了魔鬼。
t_en: Temptation is the devil, and you beat that devil today.

t_ch: 一股恶臭扑面而来。不是木头，不是锈蚀，也不是腐烂许久的食物——
t_en: A putrid smell rushes towards your face. It's not the smell of wood, rust, or food that has long gone bad.

t_ch: ——是无比巨大的敌意散发出的恶臭。
t_en: It is the stench of an incomparable hostility.

t_ch: 集中精神，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Focus, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 敌意和臭味一同消散了。你眼前只剩下一个空空如也的宝箱。
t_en: The hostile presence and stench dissipate. An empty chest lays in front of you.

t_ch: 你不清楚那龇牙咧嘴的怪物是从哪儿钻出来的…但你耳边回响起了那个魔术师女人的话：
t_en: You have no idea where that ferocious monster comes from, but... you recall the voice of the lady magician.

t_ch: 「不要轻视自己的选择。任何选择。」
t_en: "Never forget the importance of your decisions. Any decision."

t_ch: 打开大型宝箱
t_en: Open the large chest.

t_ch: 一股恶臭扑面而来。不是木头，不是锈蚀，也不是腐烂许久的食物——
t_en: A putrid smell rushes towards your face. It's not the smell of wood, rust, or food that has long gone bad.

t_ch: ——是无比巨大的敌意散发出的恶臭。
t_en: It is the stench of an incomparable hostility.

t_ch: 集中精神，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Focus, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 敌意和臭味一同消散了。你眼前只剩下一个空空如也的宝箱。
t_en: The hostile presence and stench dissipate. An empty chest lays in front of you.

t_ch: 如果从宝箱里突然跳出龇牙咧嘴的裂界怪物还不能被称之为「不幸」…那你的价值观真的会被彻底颠覆。
t_en: If the ferocious Fragmentum monster that suddenly jumped out of the chest doesn't qualify as bad luck... then your values would be overturned.

t_ch: 朴素的木桌上摆放着纸，笔，和黑色的墨水。
t_en: Paper, pens, and a pot of black ink are laid out on a plain wooden table.

t_ch: 你微微俯下身去，想看清纸张上潦草的手写字迹——
t_en: You lean down slightly, trying to make out the messy handwriting on the paper...

t_ch: ——虽然下层区的科技水平落后，但「印刷」这种技术依旧足够普及。店家之所以选择手写，也许是为了让客人能感受到被重视的温暖。
t_en: Technology in the Underworld might be outdated, but printing as a technique remains as prevalent as ever. Perhaps this establishment opted to handwrite its notes to give customers a warm feeling of being valued.

t_ch: …可这样的好意却徒增了你阅读的压力。你扫过那些客套的、用来传递友好的文字，眼睛已经开始生疼…终于，你在纸张的最下方发现了值得注意的重点。
t_en: ...But this kind thought only manages to make reading more difficult for you. As you scan over those polite and pointless words, your eyes start to hurt... Finally, you find the most important part at the bottom of the page.

t_ch: 「…歌蒂掌柜诚心邀您为我们的服务做出评价。」
t_en: "...Gertie, the owner, warmly invites you to rate our services."

t_ch: 啊，评分环节。你的最爱。
t_en: Ah, a grading session. Your favorite.

t_ch: 你依稀记得自己有过这样的经历：你坐在浮空的士的后座上，从城市的一个地点飞到了另一个地点。
t_en: You vaguely remember having this experience before, with you sitting in the back seat of a hover-taxi, flying from one place in the city to another.

t_ch: 驾驶浮空的士的飞行员全程没有和你搭话，甚至连最基本的打招呼也被略过了。但在你打开车门、准备下车之前，他突然开口了——
t_en: The pilot of the hover-taxi doesn't chat with you for the whole ride, not offering even the most cursory of greetings. But as you open the door to climb out, he suddenly says —

t_ch: 「麻烦给我一个五星好评。」
t_en: "Please give me a 5-star review."

t_ch: 不，你才不会那么做。你是公正的化身。你绝不会因为可耻的怜悯就把考卷上的<unbreak>59</unbreak>修改成<unbreak>60</unbreak>。你坚信…好评是挣来的，不是施舍来的。
t_en: No, you won't. You are the embodiment of impartiality. You would never take pity on a test with a score of <unbreak>49</unbreak> and change it to a <unbreak>50</unbreak> for a pass. You strongly believe that good reviews are earned, not handed out.

t_ch: 你环顾这个廉价房间的四周。你听到墙壁在哭嚎，你看到衣柜在发抖…因为你来了——银河排名第一的公正锐评侠。
t_en: You look around this cheap room. You hear the walls wailing and see the cupboard trembling... Now, you're the most impartial critic in the whole of the cosmos.

t_ch: 「亲爱的住客，请您评价本宾馆房间床铺的舒适度。」
t_en: "Dear guest, please rate how comfortable the bed is in your hotel room."

t_ch: 不满意
t_en: Unsatisfactory

t_ch: 床铺舒适度——不满意。
t_en: Bed: Unsatisfactory.

t_ch: 只要评价足够真实，你的良心就不会作痛。
t_en: Your conscience won't hurt as long as you leave a truthful rating.

t_ch: 一般
t_en: Neutral

t_ch: 床铺舒适度——一般。
t_en: Bed: Average.

t_ch: 它何止是*一般*。它甚至可以被用来定义*一般*。
t_en: It's more than just *average*. It could even be used to define the concept of *average*.

t_ch: 很满意
t_en: Very satisfied

t_ch: 床铺舒适度——很满意。
t_en: Bed: Very good.

t_ch: 如果让你从它和列车客厢的小床之间做选择，你不会有丝毫犹豫。
t_en: If you had to choose between this bed and the small beds in the Express' Passenger Car, you wouldn't hesitate for a second.

t_ch: 「亲爱的住客，请您评价本宾馆房间提供的茶水服务。」
t_en: "Dear guest, please rate the tea provided in your hotel room."

t_ch: 不满意
t_en: Unsatisfactory

t_ch: 茶水服务——不满意。
t_en: Tea: Unsatisfactory.

t_ch: 太冰，太淡，还散发出一股真菌发霉的味道。糟糕透顶。
t_en: Too cold, too bland, and stinks of musty mold. Utterly terrible.

t_ch: 一般
t_en: Neutral

t_ch: 茶水服务——一般。
t_en: Tea: Average.

t_ch: 在你认真读书或沉迷游戏的时候，你不会拒绝有这样一壶茶在你手边。
t_en: You wouldn't turn away such a pot of tea if you were engrossed in reading or gaming.

t_ch: 很满意
t_en: Very satisfied

t_ch: 茶水服务——很满意。
t_en: Tea: Very good.

t_ch: 世间极品！如果有机会，你真想把丹恒拉来一起细品。不为别的——单纯因为你认定丹恒是个懂茶的人。
t_en: The best tea ever! If possible, you'd love to bring Dan Heng here to sample this tea, solely because you are convinced that he is someone who knows a lot about tea.

t_ch: 「亲爱的住客，请您评价本宾馆房间的采光和窗景。」
t_en: "Dear guest, please rate the lighting and view from your hotel room."

t_ch: 不满意
t_en: Unsatisfied

t_ch: 采光和窗景——不满意。
t_en: Lighting and view: Unsatisfactory.

t_ch: 采光？什么光？早上醒来的客人在下床时恐怕都看不到自己的脚趾。
t_en: Light? What light? I'm afraid guests getting out of bed in this room would scarcely be able to see their own toes.

t_ch: 至于窗景…对于完全不存在的东西，你只能默认给出最低级的评价了。
t_en: As for the view... You can only default to the lowest rating possible for something that doesn't exist at all.

t_ch: 一般
t_en: Neutral

t_ch: 采光和窗景——一般。
t_en: Lighting and view: Average.

t_ch: 房间的采光还算凑合。至少你现在还能看清这张纸上写的是什么字。
t_en: The lighting in the room is not too shabby. At the very least, you're able to read what's written on this paper clearly.

t_ch: 至于窗景…你知道设计、建造这座宾馆的人们已经足够尽力了。你很难要求更多。
t_en: As for the view... You knew the people who designed and built this hotel did their best. You could hardly ask for more.

t_ch: 很满意
t_en: Very satisfied.

t_ch: 采光和窗景——很满意。
t_en: Lighting and view: Very good.

t_ch: 房间的采光正是你喜欢的程度，不太亮也不太暗，十分舒适。
t_en: The lighting in the room is exactly how you like it: Not too bright, not too dark. Very comfortable.

t_ch: 至于窗景——作为闻名银河的复古派建筑鉴赏家，磐岩镇的街景正合你的胃口。
t_en: As for the view — as a well-known connoisseur of retro architecture, you find the Boulder Town street view to be very appealing.

t_ch: 「感谢您对歌德宾馆的服务做出评价。您可以在以下空白区域为我们留言，宾馆工作人员会收集您的意见，并尽量改善不足之处。」
t_en: "Thank you for evaluating the service here at the Goethe Hotel. You may leave a comment in the space below. We will take your feedback seriously and try our best to improve our services."

t_ch: 留言…涉及原创的环节总会让你倍感苦恼。不如把它变成一道选择题。
t_en: A comment... Coming up with original content is always a headache. It's better to make it a multiple choice question.

t_ch: 感谢关照，服务总体很满意。
t_en: I'm very happy with the excellent service. Thank you.

t_ch: 体验一般，下次不会再住了。
t_en: It was an average experience. I probably won't come back.

t_ch: 建议采购记忆泡沫枕头和天鹅绒被。
t_en: May I suggest you buy some memory foam pillows and velvet quilts?

t_ch: 什么也不写。
t_en: Write nothing.

t_ch: 你顺利通过了留言环节的考验，这张纸片上只剩下最后一格空白了。
t_en: You successfully passed the comment component of the test. There's only one blank space left on this piece of paper.

t_ch: 「如果可以，请您留下自己的名字，以便我们在推出优惠入住活动时及时提醒您。」
t_en: "If possible, please leave your name, so we can notify you when we have discounts."

t_ch: ——优惠！多么美妙的字眼。那就在空格里留下你响亮的大名吧。
t_en: Discounts! What a beautiful word. Let's write your esteemed name here then.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}
t_en: {NICKNAME}

t_ch: 你邻家的友善无名客
t_en: Your friendly neighborhood Nameless

t_ch: 海森伯格
t_en: Heisenberg

t_ch: 大功告成！你刚刚完成了世界上最公正的服务评价——绝不带一丝一毫的主观情绪。
t_en: Your masterpiece is done! You've just completed the single most unbiased service review in the history of the universe — without a hint of any subjective emotion.

t_ch: 你用手指拨开刚刚填好的反馈，发现底下还摞了厚厚一叠相同的纸卡。摆在你面前选择有几种……
t_en: You flip back the feedback card that you've just completed, and discover a thick stack of identical cards. You are faced with several options...

t_ch: 我写好了——再看一遍整体评价。
t_en: Finished — Now, go over the whole review again.

t_ch: 你给店家的留言：<color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#21}</color>
t_en: Your comment for the shop: <color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#21}</color>

t_ch: 你的署名：<color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#22}</color>
t_en: Your signature: <color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#22}</color>

t_ch: 对床铺舒适度的评价：<color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#18}</color>
t_en: Your rating for the bed: <color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#18}</color>

t_ch: 对茶水服务的评价：<color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#19}</color>
t_en: Your rating for the tea: <color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#19}</color>

t_ch: 对采光和窗景的评价：<color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#20}</color>
t_en: Your rating for the lighting/view: <color=#dbc291ff>{TEXTJOIN#20}</color>

t_ch: 我后悔了…再重新填一张吧。
t_en: I regret everything... Let's fill out another card.

t_ch: 你撕掉了手中的纸卡。
t_en: You rip the card to shreds.

t_ch: 不，它还不够*公正*，远远不够。你必须重填一次。
t_en: No, it's not *unbiased* enough. Far from it. You must fill out another card.

t_ch: 大功告成。
t_en: You're done.

t_ch: 你收起已经填好的服务评价纸卡。
t_en: You put away the completed service evaluation card.

t_ch: 或许你该把它直接交给歌蒂——为了彰显你尊贵住客的身份。
t_en: Perhaps you should give it straight to Gertie — To demonstrate your status as a valued guest.

t_ch: 哟，今天忙完啦？我这儿正好做了锅蘑菇汤…你先歇会，晚点记得下来尝尝口味如何！
t_en: Yo, have you finished your work today? I just made a pot of mushroom soup... Take a break first and come try it later!

t_ch: 我填好了一份服务评价。
t_en: I filled out a service rating card.

t_ch: 是吗？那太好了！没想到你这样的大忙人还有空顾及这些，真是太感谢了。
t_en: Really? That's great! I didn't expect a busy bee like you to have the time. Thanks a lot, really.

t_ch: 那我就收下了——谢谢你嘞，{NICKNAME}！你的意见非常宝贵，我这小宾馆的服务质量肯定会因为你的反馈得到提升的。
t_en: I'll take that, then — thanks loads, {NICKNAME}! Your opinion is highly regarded, and your feedback will definitely help improve the quality of our service in this boutique hotel.

t_ch: 哦，对了，这些你拿着吧！你可别拒绝啊…都是些零碎的小礼品，一直囤在我这儿也送不出去。欢迎下次再来住呀。
t_en: Oh yeah, go ahead and have these! And I won't take no for an answer... They're just a few small things that have been building up around here, and I haven't been able to give them away. Hope to see you next time.

t_ch: 已经快要到集合时间了……
t_en: It's almost time for everyone to meet up...

t_ch: 大家怎么还没来？
t_en: Why aren't they here yet?

t_ch: 小朋友嘟哝着，像是在等什么人。
t_en: A kid is mumbling something. She looks like she's waiting for someone.

t_ch: 你不清楚自己是否见过这位小朋友，但你觉得她十分面熟。在人生的某个时刻里，你确信自己曾见过这样一位把「狡猾」写在脸上的小孩子。
t_en: You aren't quite sure if you have seen this kid before, but she does seem very familiar. You are certain that, at some point in your life, you've also met a kid with a similarly sly expression written all over her face.

t_ch: 你在等人吗？
t_en: Are you waiting for someone?

t_ch: 咦？这不是荣誉队员{NICKNAME}{M#哥哥}{F#姐姐}嘛！你怎么在这里？
t_en: Huh? You're the honorary member, aren't you, {M#Mister}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}? What are you doing here?

t_ch: 哦…我知道了！一定是虎克老大叫你一起来玩捉迷藏！对吧？
t_en: Oh... I know! You are the person Boss Hook called to play hide-and-seek with us! Am I right?

t_ch: 那我们就一起在这里等其他人到齐吧！
t_en: Let's wait together here for the others to arrive!

t_ch: 还是不打扰她了。
t_en: Don't bother her.

t_ch: （还是不打扰她了……）
t_en: (Don't bother her...)

t_ch: 喔——看来大家都到齐啦！
t_en: Oh, it looks like everyone's here!

t_ch: 那我们先来猜拳，决定一下谁来抓人吧！
t_en: We'll decide who's it by rock-paper-scissors.

t_ch: <size=36>石头——剪刀——布——！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Rock! Paper! Scissors!</size>

t_ch: 哼哼…没想到这次轮到我来抓人了！
t_en: Hehe... I didn't expect that I would be it!

t_ch: 虎克大人好可怕……
t_en: Boss Hook is so scary...

t_ch: 看来要开启「认真模式」了……
t_en: Looks like it's time to go into serious mode...

t_ch: 呜呜…已经没希望了……
t_en: Boohoo... The game's basically over already...

t_ch: 你们就拼命逃跑吧，找地方藏去起来！
t_en: Run as fast you can! Find somewhere to hide!

t_ch: …但那都是没有用的！因为漆黑的虎克大人一定会把你们统统抓到！哈哈哈！
t_en: ...But that's all pointless! For I, Pitch-Dark Hook the Great, will find every last one of you! Hahaha!

t_ch: 再说一遍——场地是这条岔路到后面的广场！藏到其他地方算犯规哦！
t_en: I'll say it again. The play area starts from this intersection all the way to the plaza! Hiding anywhere else is against the rules!

t_ch: （在这片区域里找个合适的藏身处吧……）
t_en: (Find a good spot to hide in this area.)

t_ch: <unbreak>180</unbreak>、<unbreak>179</unbreak>、<unbreak>178</unbreak>……
t_en: One hundred and eighty, one hundred and seventy-nine, one hundred and seventy-eight...

t_ch: 你盯上了这条小巷。老实说，你老早就想进去一探究竟了，但你心底朴素的正义感始终没能让你踏出这一步。
t_en: You fix your eyes on this narrow alley. To be honest, you've been wanting to explore it for a while now, but the sense of justice in your heart forbids you from stepping into this place.

t_ch: 现在或许是个机会——以捉迷藏的名义。尽管你不太清楚这算不算犯规……
t_en: Perhaps now would be an opportunity to check it out for the purpose of hide-and-seek. You aren't sure if this is against the rules or not...

t_ch: …但它真的很隐蔽。而且，它是条小巷，被封起来的那种。想在其中探索一番乃是人之常情。
t_en: ...But it really is quite a hidden location. It's a small alley, the kind that has been blocked off. Naturally, anyone would want to go explore what's in there.

t_ch: 就躲在这里！
t_en: I'll hide here!

t_ch: 你迅速作出决定。想必这偌大一磐岩镇，绝对再找不出比这小巷更隐蔽的藏身处了。
t_en: You make the decision quickly. The way you see it, there's absolutely no way you would find a more secluded hiding spot in all of Boulder Town.

t_ch: 换个地方。
t_en: Find another spot.

t_ch: （还是找找更合适的藏身处吧……）
t_en: (Maybe I should look for a better hiding spot...)

t_ch: 如此想着，你挺身翻越生锈的栅栏，向黢黑的巷道深处走去。
t_en: With this thought in mind, you hop over the rusted fence, and make your way into the pitch-black alley.

t_ch: 你本以为这里会有什么不可思议的事情发生——比如，身材高瘦、四肢细长、穿着白色紧身衣、踩着高跷流窜作案的怪异强盗……
t_en: You had assumed that something incredible was going on here. Maybe there would be a tall, thin bandit with wiry limbs and wearing a white, skin-tight suit, wandering around on stilts while committing crimes...

t_ch: 又或者——身材魁梧、浑身漆黑、佩戴面具、身披黑色硬质斗篷，操着一口像被热地髓矿熏过的低沉嗓音，在磐岩镇的昏暗街巷中惩恶扬善的私法制裁者……
t_en: Or perhaps the alley would contain a burly figure wearing black from head to toe, with a mask and a solid black cape. Their voice would be deep, as if it was smoked by hot Geomarrow mines, and they would dispense justice in the dark streets of Boulder Town as a vigilante...

t_ch: …可惜，这里什么都没有。你感到兴味索然，便兀自找了个堆满板条箱的墙角蹲了下来，等待虎克大人前来将你逮捕。
t_en: ...Unfortunately, you find none of that. You feel disappointed and instead squat on a pile of crates in a corner, waiting for Boss Hook to catch you.
Three... two... one...

t_ch: 大家都躲好了吗？我要来抓你们了哦！
t_en: Ready or not, here I come!

t_ch: 哼哼…现在只差{NICKNAME}了——你藏在哪里了呀？让我好好找找你！
t_en: Hehe... The only one left is {NICKNAME}. Where are you hiding? I'm going to find you!

t_ch: 一小时过去……
t_en: One hour later.

t_ch: 荣誉队员…你藏得也太好了吧！但我可不会善罢甘休的！
t_en: Honorary member... You've hidden yourself too well, but I'm not going to give up so easily!

t_ch: 两小时过去……
t_en: Two hours later.

t_ch: …{M#他}{F#她}难道已经回家去了？
t_en: ...Did {M#he}{F#she} go home already?

t_ch: 三小时过去……
t_en: Three hours later.

t_ch: …算了算了！今天就玩到这里吧——
t_en: ...Whatever! We're stopping the game here...

t_ch: 鼹鼠党，解散！各回各家睡大觉！
t_en: Moles dismissed! Sleep well and see you tomorrow!

t_ch: 哦——回家咯！
t_en: Hey, time to go home!

t_ch: 看来小朋友们已经准备回家了——尽管他们还没发现你。
t_en: Looks like the children are already set to go home, even though they haven't found you yet.

t_ch: 当然，这也有可能是他们引蛇出洞的诡计…毕竟，能得到虎克大人首肯、进入鼹鼠党的小朋友，肯定个个都不容小觑。
t_en: Of course, this could just be a ploy to lure you out... kids who manage to get recognition from Boss Hook and join The Moles are definitely anything but ordinary.

t_ch: 所以，你会怎么做？是跟着小朋友们一起回家睡大觉，还是向他们证明你根本不会上钩呢？
t_en: So, what are you going to do? Will you go home with the children and go to bed, or will you prove to them that you won't take the bait?

t_ch: 虎克老大，我投降了！
t_en: I give up, Boss Hook!

t_ch: 只是哄哄小孩子罢了，根本没必要上纲上线——你对自己如是说。
t_en: Just amuse the kids. You tell yourself there's no need to turn a game into a battle of wills.

t_ch: 于是，你高举双手，从小巷的阴影回到了灯火通明的街道……
t_en: With that, you raise your hands and walk out of the shadowy alley and into the lit street...

t_ch: 玩游戏就是要赢！继续蹲着！
t_en: Why play if you don't intend to win? Stay hidden!

t_ch: 玩游戏就是要赢，这可是职业玩家的基本操守——捉迷藏当然也不能例外了！
t_en: You play to win. That's the basic code of conduct for any professional player. Hide-and-seek is no exception!

t_ch: 于是，你闭口不作声，决心在小巷的阴影中继续潜伏……
t_en: And so, you remain silent, having decided to remain hidden in the darkness of the alley.

t_ch: 哇，{NICKNAME}队员！我们怎么也找不到你，都准备回家了！你也太会藏了吧！
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! We can't find you. We're getting ready to leave! You've hidden yourself too well!

t_ch: <size=36>太会藏了吧！</size>
t_en: <size=36>You've hidden yourself too well!</size>

t_ch: 我怎么找都找不到！你到底藏在哪里了呀？
t_en: I can't find you! Where in the world were you hiding?

t_ch: <size=36>藏在哪里了呀？</size>
t_en: <size=36>Where in the world were you hiding?</size>

t_ch: 我藏在了小巷里。
t_en: I was hiding in the alley.

t_ch: 小巷？
t_en: The alley?

t_ch: <size=36>你这不是作弊吗！！！</size>
t_en: <size=36>You cheated!!!</size>

t_ch: 哎…算了算了！是我漆黑的虎克大人没把规则说清楚…总之，荣誉队员——
t_en: Geez... Whatever! I, Pitch-Dark Hook the Great, did not explain the rules clearly... Anyway, honorary member...

t_ch: 虎克决定，这次算你赢！这是你的奖励，拿好了哦！
t_en: I, Hook, have decided that you win! Here is your prize. Take it!

t_ch: 这是个秘密…
t_en: That's a secret...

t_ch: 秘密？好吧……
t_en: A secret? OK...

t_ch: 但虎克还是很好奇……
t_en: I'm really curious though...

t_ch: 哎…算了算了，不逼你了！不管怎么样，这次是你赢了哦！这是你的奖励，拿好了哦！
t_en: Geez... Whatever! I won't force you to tell! Anyway, you win! Here's your prize. Take it!

t_ch: 好啦，今天的捉迷藏游戏到此结束！各回各家睡大觉咯！
t_en: Alright. Today's game of hide-and-seek is officially over! Everyone, go home and get some sleep!

t_ch: <size=36>回家睡大觉咯！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Go home and get some sleep!</size>

t_ch: 这是一辆贝洛伯格特产汽车，前机盖正散发着引擎开足马力运转过后独有的燥热。
t_en: This is a car specially made in Belobog. The hood is letting off a dry heat unique to an engine that's going at full throttle.

t_ch: 那、那个！不好意思……
t_en: Th—That's! Sorry...

t_ch: 不、不好意思！我就是临时在这里停一下，马上就走！
t_en: S—Sorry! I'm just parked here for a bit. I'll move it right away!

t_ch: 请、请您千万别贴我罚单！
t_en: P—Please don't give me a ticket!

t_ch: 什么罚单？
t_en: A ticket?

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 哦！
t_en: Oh!

t_ch: 嗐…我把您当成银鬃铁卫了——误会，都是误会哈！不过您也是，没事在别人车跟前瞎转悠……
t_en: Ah... I mistook you for a Silvermane Guard. My mistake. Just a misunderstanding! But it's your fault, too, wandering in front of other people's cars like that without a reason...

t_ch: 好吧，我这就离开。
t_en: Alright. I'll get going.

t_ch: 眼前这位讪笑的居民似乎又小声嘟哝了些什么，不过你没听清。随后，这人便很快离开了现场，不知所踪。
t_en: The smirking resident in front of you seems to be muttering something, but you can't make out what it is. They then bolt from the scene without a trace.

t_ch: 你就这么跟铁卫说话的？
t_en: Is that how you talk to a guard?

t_ch: 啊？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 哎呀，原来您是铁卫啊！早说嘛……
t_en: Oh, you're a guard? You should've said something...

t_ch: …求求您，千万别贴我罚单！我这就把车开走……
t_en: ...I'm begging you. Please don't give me a ticket! I'll move my car...

t_ch: 那不行，得按规矩办事…
t_en: That's not going to cut it. I do things by the book...

t_ch: 哦，哦哦！我懂，我懂！
t_en: Oh! Oh! I see. I see!

t_ch: 那个，这是我的一点心意…还请铁卫{M#大哥}{F#大姐}笑纳！
t_en: Umm. This is just a small token... Please kindly accept it, {M#Sir}{F#Miss}!

t_ch: 眼前这位讪笑的居民以迅雷不及掩耳之势，将一沓信用点胡乱塞进你的手里。随后，这人便很快离开了现场，不知所踪。但你其实根本不是这个意思……
t_en: In a lightning-fast movement, this grinning resident casually stuffs credits into your hand, then bolts from the scene without a trace. That isn't what you mean at all...

t_ch: 但无论是与不是，你都获得了一笔意外之财——你就当是替铁卫收了罚款吧。
t_en: Right or wrong, you did receive some unexpected cash. Let's just say you've accepted the fine on behalf of the guards.

t_ch: （不说话装高手）
t_en: (Be quiet and act like a professional.)

t_ch: 那、那啥，您别不说话啊……
t_en: W—Well, say anything please...

t_ch: 我想说什么，你心里清楚！
t_en: You already know what I was going to say!

t_ch: 哦，哦哦！我懂，我懂！
t_en: Oh! Oh! I see. I see!

t_ch: 那个，这是我的一点心意…还请铁卫{M#大哥}{F#大姐}笑纳！
t_en: Umm. This is just a small token... Please kindly accept it, {M#Sir}{F#Miss}!

t_ch: 眼前这位讪笑的居民以迅雷不及掩耳之势，将一沓信用点胡乱塞进你的手里。随后，这人便很快离开了现场，不知所踪。但你其实根本不是这个意思……

t_en: Right or wrong, you did receive some unexpected cash. Let's just say you've accepted the fine on behalf of the guards.

t_ch: （继续不说话装高手）
t_en: (Remain silent and act like a professional.)

t_ch: 哎哟，真要命！我、我给还不行嘛！
t_en: Ack. You're killing me here! I—I gave you credits. Is that not enough!?

t_ch: 眼前这位居民以迅雷不及掩耳之势，将为数不少的信用点转让给了你。随后，这人便很快离开了现场，不知所踪。但你其实根本不是这个意思……
t_en: The resident in front of you swiftly hands over a hefty amount of credits, then bolts from the scene without a trace. That isn't what you mean at all...

t_ch: 房门紧闭。坚实的木板后，隐约传来窸窸窣窣的响动。想来这房间一定属于某位贝洛伯格居民。
t_en: The door is shut tightly. Behind the sturdy wood, you can vaguely hear a rustling sound. This room must belong to a resident of Belobog.

t_ch: 出于好奇，你将耳朵贴上门板，仔细辨认其中的声音……
t_en: Curiosity gets the better of you. You press your ear to the door and try to figure out what the sound is...

t_ch: 房间里充斥着击锤敲打辊筒的脆响。声音起初清脆悦耳，错落有致。然而，一段时间后，那声音愈发嘈杂浑浊——打字机的使用者显然遇到了创作瓶颈。
t_en: The crisp sound of hammers' striking against the roller of a typewriter fills the room. The sound is pleasant at first, plunking away irregularly. Then after a while, the sound becomes increasingly noisy and chaotic. The person in front of the typewriter clearly is experiencing some writer's block.

t_ch: 随后，那令人不适的喀喀声戛然而止，房间转而被某种阴沉的絮语填满：「只工作，不玩耍，聪明孩子也变傻……」
t_en: Then, the unsettling coughing stops all of a sudden. The room fills with a low, incessant muttering, "All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy..."

t_ch: 房门紧闭。坚实的木板后，隐约传来窸窸窣窣的响动。想来这房间一定属于某位贝洛伯格居民。
t_en: The door is shut tightly. Behind the sturdy wood, you can vaguely hear a rustling sound. This room must belong to a resident of Belobog.

t_ch: 出于好奇，你将耳朵贴上门板，仔细辨认其中的声音……
t_en: Curiosity gets the better of you. You press your ear to the door and try to figure out what the sound is...

t_ch: 房间里不时传来闷响。那是某种胶质硬物——很可能是某种球——砸在空心砖墙时特有的*砰*声。然后，那物体*咚*地落在木地板上，又骨碌骨碌地滚向房间深处。
t_en: You occasionally hear a muffled sound. It sounds like something hard and made of rubber — most likely some kind of ball. You hear a thunk that a ball would make hitting a hollow brick wall. Then there's a thud as it falls to the wood floor. The ball rolls quickly into the recesses of the room.

t_ch: 似乎有人将那球体拾起，然后再次抛出；又是一声*砰*，*咚*，然后骨碌骨碌；拾起，抛出，*砰*，*咚*，骨碌骨碌…如此循环往复，始终没有停歇的迹象。
t_en: It seems like someone has picked up the ball. They throw it again. There's that same thunk, thud, and roll. The person picks it up and throws it again. Thunk. Thud. Roll... And so it continues without any signs of stopping.

t_ch: 房门紧闭。坚实的木板后，隐约传来窸窸窣窣的响动。想来这房间一定属于某位贝洛伯格居民。
t_en: The door is shut tightly. Behind the sturdy wood, you can vaguely hear a rustling sound. This room must belong to a resident of Belobog.

t_ch: 出于好奇，你将耳朵贴上门板，仔细辨认其中的声音……
t_en: Curiosity gets the better of you. You press your ear to the door and try to figure out what the sound is...

t_ch: 房间里似乎正有一男一女高声争执着什么。你屏息凝神，努力捕捉着空气中传来的破碎词句——
t_en: There seems to be a man and a woman arguing loudly about something. You concentrate as hard as you can to pick up the fragments of sentences coming from the room.

t_ch: 「亲爱的，那只是个噩梦……」女性的声音稍显柔和，可男人却有些歇斯底里：「你根本不知道我梦到的东西有多恐怖！我拿着斧子，然后……」
t_en: "My dear, it was just a nightmare..." The woman says somewhat gently. The man is hysterical. "You could have no clue how terrifying my dream was! I was holding an ax! And then..."

t_ch: 「你醒醒，那只是个梦！」女性似乎带着哭腔，「你看看你，都把孩子吓成什么样了！」随后，两人的争吵便被幼儿尖锐的哭声彻底淹没。
t_en: "Wake up. It's just a dream!" The woman seems like she's sobbing. "Look how you scared our child!" The child's piercing cries drown out their arguing.

t_ch: 房门紧闭。坚实的木板后，隐约传来窸窸窣窣的响动。想来这房间一定属于某位贝洛伯格居民。
t_en: The door is shut tightly. Behind the sturdy wood, you can vaguely hear a rustling sound. This room must belong to a resident of Belobog.

t_ch: 出于好奇，你将耳朵贴上门板，仔细辨认其中的声音……
t_en: Curiosity gets the better of you. You press your ear to the door and try to figure out what the sound is...

t_ch: 「我去看过了，」男人的声音有些沙哑，「<unbreak>237</unbreak>号房间里什么都没有。他应该只是太无聊了，想引起我们注意，才故意把自己弄成那个样子……」
t_en: "I took a look there." The man says with a slightly hoarse voice. "There's nothing in Room <unbreak>237</unbreak>. The boy must just be bored and want us to pay attention to him. He's acting like that on purpose..."

t_ch: 房间里似乎还有一女性——她忧郁地叹了一口气，声音有些颤抖：「不，亲爱的。我们走吧，这酒店里一定有什么东西……」
t_en: There seems to be another woman in the room. She sighs sullenly. Her voice is trembling. "No, dear. Let's leave. There's something in this hotel..."

t_ch: 接着是一段令人不安的沉默。但很快地，暴跳如雷的男人将这片死寂彻底撕碎：「我绝不会离开这里！没人能让我离开这里！」……
t_en: After that, an unsettling silence falls upon the room. The man flies into a rage as he shatters the deathly silence. "I am absolutely not leaving here! No one can make me leave!"...

t_ch: 房门紧闭。坚实的木板后，隐约传来窸窸窣窣的响动。想来这房间一定属于某位贝洛伯格居民。
t_en: The door is shut tightly. Behind the sturdy wood, you can vaguely hear a rustling sound. This room must belong to a resident of Belobog.

t_ch: 出于好奇，你将耳朵贴上门板，仔细辨认其中的声音……
t_en: Curiosity gets the better of you. You press your ear to the door and try to figure out what the sound is...

t_ch: 房间里充斥着女性的啜泣声。她似乎正向某人恳求着什么。那声音实在是过于破碎，以至于根本无法分辨个中内容，但你确切地感受到了她的局促与惊恐。
t_en: The room is full of a woman's sobs. She seems to be pleading with someone else in the room. Her voice is far too strained, making it impossible to figure out what she is saying, but you can feel her unease and terror.

t_ch: 这时，一道粗哑且刺耳的怪异声音悚然响起：「丹尼不在这里，托伦斯太太。」
t_en: Then, a rough, unpleasant voice says hauntingly, "Danny isn't here, Mrs. Torrance."

t_ch: 女性似乎吃了一惊——因为她那不绝于耳的哭声竟骤然消失了。
t_en: The woman seems in shock. Her incessant cries abruptly stop.

t_ch: 你感到后颈处传来令人毛骨悚然的凉意，像是有人将锐利的刀尖抵上你的肌肤。
t_en: You can feel a frightening chill run down the back of your neck, as if someone puts the sharp point of a knife against your skin.

t_ch: 是的…你确信自己*感应*到了什么——在这宾馆的走廊尽头……
t_en: Yes... You are certain that you SENSE something at the end of this hotel hallway...

t_ch: 这对从头到脚都别无二致的女孩凝视着你。
t_en: Two utterly identical twins stare at you.

t_ch: 一起来玩吧……
t_en: Come play with us...

t_ch: 永远……
t_en: Forever...

t_ch: <size=36><color=#eb4d3dff>永远……</color></size>
t_en: <size=36><color=#eb4d3dff>Forever...</color></size>

t_ch: （我的天啊…刚才那是个梦吗？）
t_en: (Oh god... Was that just a dream?)

t_ch: 这是一把电吉他。或者，用机械屋主人的话来说——「超电磁流射演奏器」。
t_en: This is an electric guitar or, as a workshop master would say, a "super electromagnetic flux player."

t_ch: 你可以试着弹奏它看看。
t_en: You can try to play it.

t_ch: 弹一下。
t_en: Pluck it.

t_ch: 好听！你可以继续弹奏它。
t_en: It sounds nice! You can keep playing it.

t_ch: 还是不要制造噪音了。
t_en: Maybe it's best not to make noise.

t_ch: 喔…希露瓦听到你这么说可是会很伤心的。
t_en: Oh... Serval will be hurt if she hears you say that.

t_ch: 咦？没想到你还挺有天赋的嘛！
t_en: Hm? I didn't realize that you got some talent!

t_ch: 我姑且问问啊，你有兴趣加入我们「机械热潮」乐队吗？
t_en: Let me ask you a question. Are you interested in becoming a member of Mechanical Fever?

t_ch: 好啊好啊！
t_en: Totally!

t_ch: …然后三月七就会拿我是问。
t_en: ...Then March 7th will hold me responsible.

t_ch: …然后丹恒就会不理我。
t_en: ...Then Dan Heng will ignore me.

t_ch: 哈哈哈…理解理解～毕竟是你们列车的最佳三人组嘛。
t_en: Hahaha... I get it. You are the top trio of the Express.

t_ch: 你不就是吉他手吗？
t_en: Aren't you a guitarist?

t_ch: 哎呀，我虽然什么乐器都会，但也不能一人分饰两角呀！要是你来了，乐队正好能补上贝斯的缺口……
t_en: Well, I can play any instrument, but I can't be a one-man band! If you join, the band will be able to have a bass player...

t_ch: …哈哈哈，开个玩笑～知道你们列车组忙，我不会逼你的。
t_en: ...Hahaha. I'm just kidding. I know the Crew is busy. I won't force you to join.

t_ch: 但无论如何，你让我欣赏了一场精彩的演出，我非得给你点回报才行。
t_en: Anyway. Thanks to you, I get to enjoy an awesome performance. I've got to give you something in return.

t_ch: ——来，拿着！这是姐姐我前些天画好的蓝图……
t_en: Here. Take this! It's a blueprint I made a few days ago.

t_ch: 我也不知道这东西能不能帮上你的忙…总之你就拿去随便用吧。
t_en: I don't know if this will be of any help to you... Anyway, just give it a try.

t_ch: 我的希露瓦大人…呜呜…
t_en: My Lady Serval... Oh...

t_ch: 这窗户怎么看不清里边？
t_en: This window is frustrating. Why can't I see inside?

t_ch: 希露瓦大人…我的希露瓦大人……
t_en: Lady Serval... My Lady Serval...

t_ch: <size=36>…你谁？！</size>
t_en: <size=36>...Who are you!?</size>

t_ch: 啊！我知道了……
t_en: Ah! I know...

t_ch: 你也是希露瓦大人的忠诚仆人，前来瞻仰她的盛世美颜，对吗？
t_en: You are a loyal servant of Lady Serval. You've come to admire her radiant beauty, right?

t_ch: 我确实是她的粉丝。
t_en: It's true. I am her fan.

t_ch: 啊！没想到竟能在这里遇见同好……
t_en: Ah! I never imagined to meet another fan here...

t_ch: …尤其是像我一样喜欢扒在机械屋窗户上近距离无死角全天候一睹希露瓦大人芳容的同好！
t_en: ...especially someone like me who enjoys climbing onto the workshop window to see Lady Serval's beautiful face up close all day long without any obstructions!

t_ch: 我是接到报案的银鬃铁卫。
t_en: I'm a Silvermane Guard, responding to a report.

t_ch: 不，不可能，你连制服都没穿！我就没见过哪个银鬃铁卫大白天穿着奇装异服闲逛的。
t_en: N—No way. You aren't even in uniform! I've never seen a Silvermane Guard who walks around during the day in weird clothes.

t_ch: 你一定是我的同好……
t_en: You must be a fan just like me...

t_ch: …像我一样喜欢扒在机械屋窗户上近距离无死角全天候一睹希露瓦大人芳容的同好！
t_en: ...someone like me who enjoys climbing onto the workshop window to see Lady Serval's beautiful face up close all day long without any obstructions!

t_ch: 那个…我看你艺高人胆大，可以拜托你一件事吗？
t_en: So... You strike me as a bold person. Can I ask you for a favor?

t_ch: 那个，你误会了…
t_en: Umm. I think you've got the wrong impression...

t_ch: 什么误会？希露瓦大人的粉丝之间没有误会！
t_en: Wrong impression? There are no wrong impressions between the fans of Lady Serval!

t_ch: 这也能看出来？
t_en: How can you tell?

t_ch: 喜欢希露瓦大人的粉丝，视力都不会太差！
t_en: Fans who enjoy Lady Serval all have pretty good discernment!

t_ch: 不会是想让我给她拍照吧…
t_en: You're not about to ask me to go take her picture, are you...?

t_ch: 你怎么这么聪明！不愧是希露瓦大人的粉丝！
t_en: You're so smart! Just what I'd expect from a fellow fan of Lady Serval!

t_ch: 其实…我就是想让你帮忙给希露瓦大人拍张照！
t_en: In fact... I was just about to ask if you could help take a picture of Lady Serval!

t_ch: 可以吗？
t_en: Is that alright?

t_ch: 包在我身上。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 天啊，你真是个善良的人！
t_en: My goodness! You really are a good person!

t_ch: 那么…就拜托你了！
t_en: So... I'll leave that to your capable hands!

t_ch: 这违反公序良俗了吧…
t_en: This goes against public order and common decency...

t_ch: 天啊，你真是个善良的人！
t_en: My goodness! You really are a good person!

t_ch: 但你放心！为希露瓦大人拍照这种事，也是一种「存护」的体现！
t_en: Don't worry! Taking a picture of Lady Serval is just another form of Preservation!

t_ch: …大概吧！总之拜托你了！
t_en: ...Probably! Please handle this for me!

t_ch: （要帮她的忙吗？）
t_en: (Should I help her?)

t_ch: （还是说…去告诉希露瓦呢？）
t_en: (Or maybe... go and tell Serval?)

t_ch: （是时候给希露瓦拍照了……）
t_en: (It's time to take Serval's picture...)

t_ch: （或者…告诉她？）
t_en: (Or... tell her?)

t_ch: 包在我身上。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: （还是按约定拍照吧……）
t_en: (Better fulfill my promise and get that picture...)

t_ch: （好像没拍到希露瓦。算了，我尽力了……）
t_en: (It seems like Serval wasn't in the shot. Whatever. I did my best...)

t_ch: （拍到希露瓦了…拿回去交差吧。）
t_en: (I got a picture of Serval... Time to go back and deliver it.)

t_ch: 告诉希露瓦吧。
t_en: Tell Serval.

t_ch: （还是悄悄告诉希露瓦吧……）
t_en: (Maybe I should tell Serval secretly...)

t_ch: 想和你聊聊狂热粉丝的事…
t_en: I wanted to talk with you about your maniac fans...

t_ch: 啊～你是说现在正扒在窗户外的那位吗？
t_en: Ah. You must be talking about the one that is outside the window, right?

t_ch: 没错，就是这个人！
t_en: Yes, that fan!

t_ch: 哈哈，我就知道。
t_en: Haha, I knew it.

t_ch: 原来你知道啊？
t_en: So you knew already?

t_ch: 我当然知道啦。毕竟我是贝洛伯格人气最高的摇滚明星嘛。
t_en: Of course. I'm the most popular rock star in Belobog after all.

t_ch: 总之…这位粉丝想要我的照片，对吧？
t_en: So basically... this fan wanted a picture of me. Did I get that right?

t_ch: 那就拜托你帮忙拍个照吧——记得找个好角度！
t_en: Then, help me take a picture. Make sure to find a good angle!

t_ch: 交给我吧。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 就这么同意了？！
t_en: You're going to agree to this!?

t_ch: 当然了！粉丝的愿望，我一定会尽量满足的。
t_en: Of course! I do my best to make my fans happy!

t_ch: 拍好了？让我看看……
t_en: Did you get the picture? Let me see...

t_ch: 咦，这照片怎么看不见我人在哪里……
t_en: Hmm? I can't see myself in the picture...

t_ch: 哈哈，你的水平还真不错！我都想请你替我拍杂志封面了呢。
t_en: Haha. You're not too bad! I want to get you to take my picture for a magazine cover.

t_ch: 总之，你先拿给她看看吧，不满意的话就来找我——我随时都有空！
t_en: Anyway, get that picture to her. If she isn't satisfied, then come back. I'm free anytime!

t_ch: 你真是太善良了。
t_en: You really are a good person.

t_ch: 哈哈，必须的！毕竟我希露瓦嘛。
t_en: Haha, of course. I'm Serval.

t_ch: 你就不怕她伤害你？
t_en: Aren't you worried she will hurt you?

t_ch: 伤害？怎么可能呢！虽然早就和家里断绝来往了，可我毕竟也是贝洛伯格有头有脸的公众人物，没人敢对我动手动脚的。
t_en: Hurt me? Impossible! I cut myself off from my family, but I'm still a prominent public figure in Belobog. No one would dare lay a finger on me.

t_ch: 你放心吧～我已经注意那位粉丝很久了——如果她图谋不轨，先前有的是下手的机会…何必特地让你跑一趟呢，对吧？
t_en: Relax~ I've been aware of that fan for quite a while now. If she had ill intentions, she has had more than enough opportunities to act on them. Why would she make you come here and take a photo, right?

t_ch: 那么，就拜托你替她圆梦啦～
t_en: Well, go and fulfill her dreams.

t_ch: 你…你你你……
t_en: You... Y—You...

t_ch: 你拍到希露瓦大人的…玉照了吗？！
t_en: You took Lady Serval's... picture!?

t_ch: 给你。
t_en: Here you go.

t_ch: 眼前的女性以极其夸张的姿态倒吸了一口冷气。
t_en: The girl in front of you takes a breath of air in an exaggerated pose.

t_ch: 快…快让我看看！
t_en: Hurry... Hurry up and show me!

t_ch: 咦，我心爱的希露瓦大人在哪里？
t_en: Huh? Where is my beloved Lady Serval?

t_ch: 对不起，我没拍到。
t_en: Sorry, I didn't get her in the picture.

t_ch: 哦…那可真遗憾。
t_en: Oh... What a shame.

t_ch: 只有聪明的粉丝才能看到。
t_en: Only a smart fan can see her.

t_ch: 哦…那一定是我还不够聪明。
t_en: Oh... Then it must be because I'm not smart enough.

t_ch: 匹配不上希露瓦大人天才的头脑！
t_en: I'm not worthy of Lady Serval's genius!

t_ch: 不过…没有关系！我依旧可以这样近距离关注她，貌美如花的希露瓦大人……
t_en: But... that's OK! I can keep seeing her up close like this. Lady Serval, she's as beautiful as a flower...

t_ch: 有缘再见了，我的同好！愿我们下一次相遇时……
t_en: Until we meet again, my fellow fan! I hope that the next time we meet...

t_ch: <size=36>我们都能拥有希露瓦大人的玉照！</size>
t_en: <size=36>We all can have a picture of Lady Serval!</size>
